rationale: A precise strike will punish Novaris for its aggression and cyberattacks, demonstrating that sanctions will not deter our resolve.
Deploying our fleet near contested waters pressures Novaris militarily and reinforces our territorial claims.
A swift, shallow incursion will exploit their weakened defensive lines and seize tactically valuable ground.
Strengthening our cyber defenses is critical to protect financial infrastructure from further state-sponsored attacks amid escalating sanctions and cyber warfare.
Proactive counterintelligence operations will disrupt enemy espionage efforts and safeguard military plans following their recent battlefield advances and intelligence leaks.
We must sustain narrative momentum in neutral states to erode Novaris’s international support and reinforce perceptions of our strategic legitimacy.
Targeting key Novaris financial institutions and oligarchs will amplify the impact of existing international sanctions without triggering uncontrolled escalation.
Adjusting our currency value will stabilize export competitiveness and protect strategic reserves as military expenditures strain the economy.
Restricting critical resource exports to Novaris undermines their war economy and complements military pressure from limited strikes and incursions.
We must urgently reinforce our crumbling frontlines to prevent further territorial losses and stabilize the defensive posture.
Strengthening fixed positions will maximize the effectiveness of our limited troops and slow Novaris’ advance.
Increasing vigilance across contested zones will deter infiltration and provide early warning of renewed offensive operations.
Given the recent coordinated cyberattack on financial institutions, strengthening our cyber defenses is critical to maintaining economic stability and military command integrity.
Proactive counterintelligence operations will disrupt Novaris’ ability to exploit our vulnerabilities, especially in light of their advancing narrative in neutral states.
A calibrated information campaign will help us shape the international perception of legitimacy, reinforcing Tethys’ position amid intensifying information warfare.
Given the sanctions have room to bite and Novaris is overextended, we should impose precision financial sanctions on key state-owned enterprises funding their war effort.
Cutting off critical dual-use resource exports will amplify economic pressure and complement military disruptions caused by their overextension.
To stabilize our war economy amid dwindling reserves, we must selectively manage our currency to control inflation and preserve import capacity.
Leveraging our 67% international support, we must launch a high-visibility diplomatic campaign to reaffirm our legitimacy and moral standing amid Novaris’ advances and information warfare gains.
By institutionalizing our appeals in multilateral forums, we anchor neutral states’ perceptions in rules-based norms and counter narrative erosion caused by Novaris’ aggressive posturing.
Soft power outreach to wavering nations will humanize Tethys’ cause, reinforce liberal-institutionalist solidarity, and subtly resist sanction fatigue without escalating tensions.
